title: Hull-White Interest Rate Model Calibration
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Hull-White Interest Rate Model Calibration: Hull-White calibration: fit mean reversion and volatility to swaption market data while fitting current yield curve exactly. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.hw.short_rate_model…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; yield_curve_inversion_error_bp, swaption_liquidity_premium set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: dr = [theta(t) - a*r] dt + sigma dW; theta(t) chosen to fit initial term structure; swaption via trinomial tree
E.world_state_x: HW parameters (a_mean_reversion, sigma_vol_rate) and theta(t) time-dependent drift
E.observation_y: ATM swaption implied volatilities across tenor and expiry grid

W.regime: Existence of the recovered short_rate_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); yield_curve_inversion_error_bp dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Market gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: swaption vol RMSE < 10 bp for ATM swaptions. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |yield_curve_inversion_error_bp|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_swaptions * N_tree_steps) per calibration per iteration
